title: pteridophyta
intro: The pteridophyta division rank is a group of vascular plants that have true roots, stems, and leaves, but do not produce flowers or seeds, and are characterized by the production of spores for reproduction. They are commonly known as ferns and their allies. Other examples of plant divisions include monocotyledonae, which comprises plants with one seed leaf, magnoliopsida, a group of flowering plants that have two seed leaves, and polypodiopsida, a division of vascular plants that includes ferns and their relatives. The pteridophyta division is a distinct group within the plant kingdom, with its own unique characteristics and features.

classes.class_desc: Polypodiopsida is a class of vascular plants commonly known as ferns.
